title: Lesson Quiz: Professional Communication Skills
description: Assessment of the concept that Formal communication, tone, and clarity.

choices.text: Communication that is clear, respectful, and appropriate for formal settings
choices.feedback: Correct. Professional communication should be clear, respectful, and appropriate for the setting.
Professional communication should consider the audience and purpose.
Professional communication should avoid slang and overly casual language.

choices.text: Communication meant only for close friends
choices.feedback: Professional communication is used in formal school or workplace situations.

questions.prompt: A formal tone is usually more appropriate than slang in a professional email.

choices.feedback: Professional emails should usually use formal, respectful language instead of slang.
Correct. A formal tone is more appropriate in professional communication.

questions.prompt: Which message opening sounds most professional?

choices.feedback: This opening is too casual for a professional setting.

choices.text: yo can you fix this for me
choices.feedback: This opening is too informal and not respectful enough for professional communication.

choices.text: Hello, I am writing to ask for help with my project
choices.feedback: Correct. This opening is respectful, clear, and professional.
This opening is unclear and too informal.

questions.prompt: Why does clarity matter in professional communication?

choices.text: Because it allows a message to stay vague
choices.feedback: Professional communication should be clear, not vague.

choices.text: Because it removes the need for respectful tone
choices.feedback: Clarity and respectful tone both matter.

choices.text: Because the reader can understand the message more easily
choices.feedback: This idea is related, but it does not best explain why clarity matters to the reader.

choices.text: Because clear messages help the audience understand the purpose and request
choices.feedback: Correct. Clarity helps the audience understand what the message means and what is needed.

questions.prompt: Professional communication should be respectful even when the message is brief.

choices.feedback: Correct. Professional communication should stay respectful even in short messages.
Respectful language is important even in a brief message.
